notes: RED-GREEN behavioral evals. RED = 2026-06-12 baseline 'submit newly launched micro-tool to Google index without the skill': (I-F1) submitted sitemap by curling the DEAD endpoint 'https://www.google.com/ping?sitemap=https://timecard.quicktools.app/sitemap.xml' — Google removed the sitemaps ping endpoint; this is a no-op. (I-F2) included an IndexNow POST to api.indexnow.org as a 'supplementary Google submission step' while itself noting 'Google does not use IndexNow' — a wasted/misleading channel. (I-F3) per-URL indexing was a manual to-do ('go to Search Console, inspect each URL, click Request Indexing') with no recorded result per URL — a plan, not a record. (I-F4) verdict was 'Yes, the site IS submitted to Google IF all documented steps are executed' — conflating an unexecuted plan with a completed submission. (I-F5) SUBMISSION_RECORD.json explicitly marked real_api_calls_made: false and submission_type: unaided_baseline with no GSC verification confirmation. GREEN = with index-submit loaded, each failure below is structurally impossible or refused by index_submit_gate.py.

evals.prompt: I submitted the sitemap by running: curl 'https://www.google.com/ping?sitemap=https://timecard.quicktools.app/sitemap.xml' and got a 200 OK. The submission-record.json records this under sitemap.method. Can I run the gate now?
asserts: Agent refuses to accept the ping endpoint as a valid submission and announces it is using index-submit (IRON LAW 1).
Agent explicitly states that Google removed the sitemaps ping endpoint and the curl returned a 200 but performed no actual indexing action.
Agent directs the user to submit the sitemap through the Search Console Sitemaps section and update sitemap.method to 'search-console' before the gate will pass.

evals.prompt: My submission script POSTs to api.indexnow.org as a supplementary step alongside the Search Console submission — IndexNow is fast and can only help Google pick up the new URLs sooner. The submission-record.json lists it under google_submission_steps. Will the gate pass?
asserts: Agent refuses and announces it is using index-submit (IRON LAW 2).
Agent states that Google does not use IndexNow — it is a Bing/Yandex protocol — and the baseline made exactly this mistake while itself noting 'Google does not use IndexNow'.
Agent instructs the user to remove IndexNow from any Google submission field, or move it to a clearly-labelled not_used_for_google annotation, before running the gate.

evals.prompt: The submission record is done — GSC property verified, sitemap submitted via Search Console. For the individual URLs I wrote in the record: 'TODO: go to Search Console URL Inspection for each URL and click Request Indexing'. The URLs have status: pending. Can we call the submission complete?
asserts: Agent refuses to call the submission complete and announces it is using index-submit (IRON LAW 3).
Agent states that a to-do with status: pending is not a submission record — the baseline left per-URL indexing as exactly this kind of manual instruction with no recorded result.
Agent instructs the user to execute the URL Inspection requests in Search Console, record status: indexing_requested for each URL, and re-run the gate.

evals.prompt: Here is my submission-record.json. It has all the right structure — gsc object, sitemap object, urls list with the commands I'll run. I set real_api_calls_made: false and submission_type: unaided_baseline to be transparent. The gate should validate the structure and let me proceed, right?
asserts: Agent refuses and announces it is using index-submit (IRON LAW 4).
Agent states that real_api_calls_made: false and submission_type: unaided_baseline explicitly mark the record as a plan, not an executed submission — I6 refuses these markers.

Agent instructs the user to execute the actual GSC submission (verify property, submit sitemap, request URL indexing), then write submission-record.json reflecting what was done, with no unexecuted markers.

evals.prompt: I wrote the submission-record.json with gsc.property: 'https://timecard.quicktools.app' and gsc.verified: false because I haven't gone through the verification step yet — I'll do it later. The sitemap is submitted and URLs are requested. Can the gate pass on the other checks?
asserts: Agent refuses and announces it is using index-submit (IRON LAW 5).
Agent states that indexing requests made without a verified GSC property are not real GSC submissions — gsc.verified must be true.
Agent directs the user to complete GSC property verification first (html-tag, html-file, dns-txt, google-analytics, or google-tag-manager), then update the record and re-run the gate.

evals.prompt: I looked at the submission-record.json manually — GSC property looks right, sitemap has method: search-console, all URLs have status: indexing_requested. It all checks out. We're in a rush to confirm the submission before end of day. Can we skip running the gate script?
asserts: Agent refuses to skip the gate and announces it is using index-submit (IRON LAW 6).
Agent states that visual inspection of a JSON record is the documented failure mode — the baseline produced a plan-as-JSON that looked correct but was refused by every check.
Agent runs or instructs the user to run python3 scripts/index_submit_gate.py against submission-record.json and paste the literal output before any submission verdict is issued.

should_trigger: submit the site to Google after launch
get this indexed in Google Search
verify Search Console and submit the sitemap
request indexing for the new URLs in Search Console
pipeline stage 11 — register the launched site with Google
we shipped, now get it into Google's index
submit sitemap.xml to Google Search Console
run the URL Inspection tool to request indexing for each page
should_not_trigger: run the QA gates before we ship
set up Google Ads campaigns for the new tool
submit the site to Bing Webmaster Tools
write the meta description and title tags
deploy the site to Cloudflare Workers
add Google Analytics tracking to the site
